text: Fill in the blanks about male external genital anatomy (AN46.1-5).
questions: The testis is enclosed by the *tunica albuginea/albuginea*, which sends inward partitions called *septa testis/septa* dividing it into approximately *250/two hundred and fifty* lobules containing seminiferous tubules.
The cremasteric muscle is derived from the *internal oblique/internal oblique muscle* and functions in *temperature/thermoregulation* regulation of the testis by raising and lowering it.

Varicocele is almost always *left/left-sided* because the left testicular vein drains into the *left renal vein/renal vein* at a right angle, while the right testicular vein drains directly into the *IVC/inferior vena cava*.

The lymphatic drainage of the testis is to the *para-aortic/lumbar* nodes (not inguinal), because the testis develops *retroperitoneally/in the posterior abdominal wall* and descends through the inguinal canal guided by the *gubernaculum/gubernaculum testis*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the topic and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
